justification: All rubric criteria met: both training (weekly batch) and serving (real-time prediction service) pipelines are fully designed with clear components and data flows. The 100ms P99 constraint is addressed through in-process inference, gradient-boosted trees for sub-ms prediction, and no I/O on the hot path. Feature store is identified as necessary (implemented as shared feature-lib to prevent training-serving skew, explicitly justified as right-sized for 4-person team). Model versioning with blue-green deployment and rollback mechanism is designed. Team size is factored throughout with recommendations against heavy infrastructure (no TF Serving, no Feast, no auto-rollback). Trade-offs and risks sections are comprehensive and well-reasoned.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough two-path architecture with clear separation of serving (hot) and training (cold) paths
100ms P99 constraint drives multiple design decisions: in-process inference, gradient-boosted trees over deep learning, no I/O in hot path, caller provides all features

Feature store decision is nuanced — recommends shared library over full feature store (Feast/Tecton) specifically because of 4-person team size, while acknowledging the core requirement of training-serving parity
Blue-green model deployment with human-in-the-loop rollback is well-designed and appropriate for team size
Identifies training-serving skew as critical risk with concrete mitigation (shared feature-lib with parity integration tests)
Practical phased implementation plan with clear ownership split between ML and platform engineers
Risks section covers feature staleness, model drift, and team bus factor — all relevant concerns
Shadow scoring is mentioned but the mechanism is not fully detailed — how are shadow predictions stored and compared?
Weekly retraining frequency is stated but model drift risk mitigation could be stronger — no mention of drift detection triggering earlier retraining

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

This is a real-time ML inference system with a batch training pipeline — a well-understood pattern. The architecture separates the **serving path** (latency-critical, synchronous) from the **training path** (batch, asynchronous) with a **model registry** as the integration seam between them. Given the 4-person team and the 100ms P99 constraint, I recommend a lean architecture: pre-computed features, a lightweight model (gradient-boosted trees), and a blue-green model deploy
